title: FPT-176 Hille-on-hardware unified benchmark v2 — true spanning REVOKE

spanning_exchange_walltime.definition: Cross-kernel OBTAIN via session — VPE0→K0→DTUBridge→net→K1→VPE1 service handler→reply

spanning_revoke_session_walltime.definition: TRUE cross-kernel REVOKE — revokes session cap (Hille tier-2 path with remote children); per-iter creates fresh session at sel 700 then revokes; triggers REVOKE/REVOKEFINISH KRNLC

spanning_chain_revoke_session_10_walltim.definition: Local chain depth 10 rooted at session cap; revoke walks chain AND triggers cross-kernel REVOKE for session's remote child

exchange.interpretation: Round-trip dominated by emulated E1000 + UDP + ChaCha20-Poly1305 + VPE1 service handler

revoke.interpretation: REVOKE/REVOKEFINISH KRNLC is much cheaper than CREATESESS — no VPE1 service handler involved, just kernel-to-kernel

chain_100.interpretation: Cross-kernel REVOKE delta is constant ~50us regardless of local chain depth — confirms tier-2 fires once per spanning subtree, scales O(local_chain) + O(1) cross-kernel

key_findings: Cross-kernel REVOKE/REVOKEFINISH overhead = ~50us delta over local revoke. CONSISTENT across chain depths 10/50/100.
Hille two-tier revocation behaves as designed: tier-1 (local subtree) walks O(N), tier-2 fires ONCE per spanning subtree boundary regardless of local depth.
Spanning OBTAIN is 180x more expensive than spanning REVOKE (22.7ms vs 126us). Reason: OBTAIN involves VPE1's service-handler reply path; REVOKE is kernel-to-kernel only.
QEMU-vs-gem5 hardware-tax for REVOKE = 66x. For chain_100 = 9.3x — close to gem5 because local chain walk dominates and is similar speed on QEMU TCG.
Concurrency caveat: numbers are SINGLE-INITIATOR. Multi-initiator stress (3-node Test 14) shows 1/3 INV_ARGS rate from a protocol-level race in exchangeOverSession.
